_comment: Canonical StakeResolution document shape. One row per (tribunal_command_id, agent_id) pair, written by the reputation_service post-verdict. Records the outcome scalar fed into the agent's EMA scalar update and any slash tier applied (GDD §5-§6, §14.5). The document id is the composite `{tribunal_command_id}:{agent_id}` which gives write-once idempotency: replaying the same verdict cannot double-resolve.
_consumers: g8ee (reputation_service — writes one row per affected agent after each verdict)
g8ee (peer-auditor replay — recomputes EMA trajectory from the resolution log)
g8ee (ops/debug — grounding source for 'why did agent X's scalar change')

stake_resolution._description: Per-agent stake resolution record tied to a specific tribunal verdict. Append-only; never revised. Document id is the composite key `{tribunal_command_id}:{agent_id}` so that replaying the same verdict is a no-op.

id.description: Composite document id: `{tribunal_command_id}:{agent_id}`. Write-once idempotency guard.

investigation_id.description: Investigation that produced the verdict this resolution is attributed to.

tribunal_command_id.description: TribunalCommand whose outcome drove this stake resolution.

agent_id.description: Stable persona id whose scalar was updated (axiom, concord, variance, pragma, nemesis, sage, triage, auditor).

outcome_score.description: Scalar in [0.0, 1.0] fed into the agent's EMA update. 1.0 = best outcome for this persona given the verdict; 0.0 = worst.

rationale.description: Short machine-readable reason code (e.g. `auditor_passed`, `verification_failed_revised`, `nemesis_abstain_clean`). Grounds the scalar so peer auditors can replay.

slash_tier.description: Slash tier applied (1 = correlated/catastrophic, 2 = provable fault, 3 = liveness). Null when no slash was applied.

scalar_before.description: Agent's EMA scalar prior to this resolution.

scalar_after.description: Agent's EMA scalar after applying ema_update(outcome_score) and any slash-tier adjustment.

half_life.description: EMA half-life used for this resolution (number of resolutions for the smoothing weight to halve). Captured for replayability when the env default rotates.

created_at.description: When the resolution was written (UTC, ISO 8601 with Z suffix).
